completion_summary: Completed the admitted parametric self-model registry and invariant-based verification slice. CAP-0010 provides static specifications for all existing record families, a read-only dynamic registry over canonical JSON, registry-backed index generation, reference and lifecycle metadata, diagnostic GraphRelation-compatible normalized relations, projection-drift enforcement, fixture and production invariants, a validated collision-safe local record-creation CLI, and developer documentation for non-transactional ID allocation. Focused tests, the full repository suite, registry audit, and normalized SELF_MODEL.json audit passed. The external projection schema and runtime behavior remain unchanged.
constraints: Implementation is authorized only within the scope admitted by WARR-20260710-0011.
The proposal and admission commits must contain no registry implementation.
Existing JSON records remain the sole canonical source.
Do not introduce a second manually maintained object inventory.
Preserve the current SELF_MODEL.json external schema in this slice.
Do not add new record types, statuses, or lifecycle semantics.
Do not treat registry-backed index-to-registry count equality as independent verification.
Locally suggested IDs are advisory and non-transactional; Git merge or rebase resolves concurrent allocation collisions.
Do not add a centralized ID service or database.
Do not automatically commit or push generated records.
Do not expose CAP-0009 through additional scripts or provider flags.
Do not alter context selection, provider prompts, or runtime behavior.
Implementation must remain within WARR-20260710-0011 and requires new governance for any scope expansion.
expected_outputs: Static specifications for every existing self-model record type.
A registry-backed build_self_model_index that preserves the existing SELF_MODEL.json external schema.
Generic uniqueness, reference, lifecycle, and omission checks.
Temporary fixture repositories and lifecycle tests.
Production tests without exact global object counts or complete historical inventory assertions.
Developer documentation for registry use and ID collision handling.
mitigation: Preserve the external projection schema, use independently constructed fixture repositories and filesystem completeness checks, keep production tests invariant-based, document volatile fields and ID limitations, and deliver the work incrementally with full-suite and audit verification.
next_action: Keep GAP-0002 and GAP-0003 open. Any further CAP-0009 exposure, runtime graph-local context integration, new self-model record family, lifecycle change, or distributed ID-allocation mechanism requires a separate proposed and admitted plan.
non_goals: Creating a transaction-safe distributed ID allocator.
Adding new self-model record types or ontology concepts.
Changing existing record lifecycle states or meanings.
Automatically creating rationale, evidence, or governance decisions.
Automatically committing or pushing generated records.
Implementing CAP-0009 provider-facing or script-facing exposure.
Closing GAP-0002 or GAP-0003 in this slice.
objective: Make existing self-model record instances dynamically discoverable, typed, queryable, and verifiable without requiring code or historical test changes whenever a capability, gap, plan, verification, or warrant instance is added.
proposed_change: Introduce static record-type specifications and a dynamic SelfModelRegistry generated exclusively from existing canonical JSON records. Refactor build_self_model_index to consume that registry while preserving the current SELF_MODEL.json external schema. Add normalized projection-drift enforcement, referential-integrity and lifecycle checks, temporary fixture-repository tests, GraphRelation-compatible relation output, and an explicit create_self_model_record.py skeleton CLI with locally suggested non-transactional IDs.
rationale: COMP-0026 records independent provider agreement that the observed failures represent a distinct architectural self-model gap rather than isolated test cleanup.

Registering GAP-0003 and WARR-20260710-0010 caused failures in hard-coded open-gap lists and counts, recommendation inventories, supported-warrant counts, and the complete warrant-ID set even though the generated index and normalized audit were correct.
Existing JSON records must remain the sole canonical source; a second manually maintained inventory would reproduce the same drift problem.
Instance addition should become zero-code, while adding a new record type should remain an explicit ontology change requiring schema, validator, specification, documentation, and focused tests.
Completing this prerequisite before further CAP-0009 exposure reduces repository fragility without cancelling GAP-0002.
risk: A registry refactor could create false confidence if discovery, index generation, and tests all depend on the same implementation path. It could also accidentally change the SELF_MODEL.json schema, omit files, centralize ontology logic incorrectly, or imply concurrency guarantees for ID allocation that do not exist.
scope: Define static record-type specifications for existing self-model record kinds, including directory, filename and ID rules, ID field, validator, statuses, and lifecycle semantics.
Implement a read-only dynamic registry that discovers and validates all existing self-model JSON records.
Expose typed lookup, filtering, counts, locally suggested next IDs, reference resolution, and normalized relations.
Keep normalized relation output identical or losslessly compatible with GraphRelation fields: source_id, predicate, target_id, and authoritative.
Refactor build_self_model_index to use the registry while preserving the current SELF_MODEL.json external schema.
Add a normalized comparison that fails audit or CI when committed SELF_MODEL.json differs from the current repository projection, excluding only explicitly documented volatile fields.
Move known-count and lifecycle-transition testing into temporary fixture repositories with independently known contents.
Replace production exact-count and complete-inventory assertions with validation, uniqueness, referential-integrity, lifecycle, omission-detection, and projection-drift invariants.
Add a schema-valid record-skeleton CLI that suggests a locally available max-suffix ID, refuses current-worktree collisions, validates before writing, and documents merge-time collision handling.
success_criteria: The plan validates through the current self-model schema.
The plan is indexed as admitted and open.
The admitted plan references WARR-20260710-0011 and contains no completion fields until implementation is independently verified.
No implementation files change in the proposal commit.
After admitted implementation, adding an instance of an existing record type requires no Python or test-source change.
Adding a new record type remains a deliberate ontology change requiring a validator or schema, one type specification, documentation, and focused tests.
Fixture tests independently verify known counts, omissions, invalid references, and lifecycle transitions.
Production tests contain no exact global quantities or complete historical ID inventories.
Audit or CI fails when committed SELF_MODEL.json differs from a normalized fresh projection outside documented volatile fields.
Normalized relations are losslessly compatible with GraphRelation.
The skeleton CLI refuses local collisions, validates before writing, and clearly documents that allocation is not concurrency-safe.
Self-model tests, the full test suite, and self-model audit pass.
summary: Admit a governed implementation slice that replaces brittle production-inventory assertions with a parametric self-model registry, fixture-based lifecycle tests, repository invariants, and explicit generated-projection drift detection. Implementation is authorized only within the scope of WARR-20260710-0011.
title: Introduce parametric self-model registry and invariant-based verification
